raw_output:
PR #139 review (Claude, Fable 5), feat/113-monthly-nights 7f332ab vs origin/dev (6 files, +281/-24). Verification in scratch worktree, exit codes: backend pytest 556 exit 0 (coverage 95.28%), mypy 0, ruff 0, frontend vitest 314/45 exit 0, cold-cache eslint 0, prettier 0, build 0. FINDINGS: C1 MEDIUM — 'slept 11:42 PM' label dishonest: SleepRecord.bedtime is Oura bedtime_start (time into bed); sleep onset = bedtime_start + latency (stored separately as onset_latency_minutes); label wrong by 10-30 min nightly; everywhere else the app calls this 'Bedtime'. C2 LOW — weekday (from rec.date, night-ending Oura day convention, matches the displayed date) juxtaposed with a pre-midnight clock time invites 'Tuesday - 11:42 PM' to read as Tuesday evening when it was Monday evening; after-midnight starts genuinely on the labeled day; midnight/noon edges verified and tested. C3 NIT — 'SPA and HTML export render identically' overstated (weekday.slice(0,3) vs full; Python round-half-even vs JS half-up at .5). C4 NIT — test_bedtime_formats_across_midnight_boundaries is a pure unit test sitting in TestMonthlyReport with an unused db fixture and in-function import. VERIFIED CLEAN: ADR 003 sparse rendering (omit-when-NULL both sides, join-based separators, no dangling separators for any combination; deep=0 renders 'deep 0m' — recorded zero is data; 'None' not in html asserted); 'HRV ' sentinel sound (tables use bare HRV cells, only the night line emits 'HRV '); NightSummary consumed only by MonthlyReportResponse best/worst_night (response_model set); all new fields optional-with-defaults in Pydantic and TS, legacy-payload test present; .report-muted CSS exists.

--- Post-review delta ---
Fix round commit 2d32e28: C1 fixed — label 'slept' -> 'bed' in _night_html and SPA nightDetails (both surfaces read e.g. 'bed 11:42 PM'); C3 fixed — _night_summary docstring softened to 'render the same fields' (SPA abbreviates weekday; avg_hrv rounding may differ at .5). Test sentinels hardened: null-fields sentinel is now re.search(r'bed \d', html) is None — probed against real rendered HTML including the 'Pre-bed ritual' factor tag, which does NOT match, while the real label does. Delta scope: report_service.py, test_report_service.py, BestWorstNightsCard.tsx + test — all within the lane touch-set (BestWorstNightsCard substitution recorded in the wave plan). Post-fix verification: backend pytest 556 exit 0 (coverage 97.91%), mypy 0, ruff 0; frontend cold-cache lint 0, format:check 0, vitest 314/45 files exit 0, build 0.

findings.summary: 'slept <time>' mislabels time-into-bed (Oura bedtime_start) as sleep onset
findings.reason: label -> 'bed <time>' on both surfaces; assertions + robust sentinels updated (2d32e28)

findings.summary: Weekday + pre-midnight bedtime juxtaposition invites off-by-one-evening misreading
findings.reason: rec.date is the night-ending Oura day convention used repo-wide and matches the date the card already shows; 'bed' labeling reduces ambiguity; changing day attribution would diverge from every other surface
softened to 'render the same fields' with the concrete caveats (2d32e28)

findings.summary: Pure unit test placed in TestMonthlyReport with unused db fixture
findings.reason: cosmetic; test is correct and load-bearing where it is
